Table of contents

  • Description:
    • 1. Set up R environment for Paleogeographic reconstructions
    • 2. Download Paleo-Data
    • 3. Identify Madagascar and Africa in Paleo reconstructions
    • 4. Calculate Area and Distances
    • 5. Convert to FIG-model format

Get Paleo Biogeographic data for Africa and Madagascar

Author

Friederike Wölke

Published

June 26, 2026

Description:

In order to use biogeoraphic features of Africa and Madagascar to build our feature-informed biogeogeraphic (FIG) model in RevBayes we need some information about our study areas back in time. These features are: - Area (km^2) - Distance between Areas (km)

Luckily there are paleo datasets freely available through the chronosphere R package that we can use with the rgplates R package to reconstruct paleocostlines back in time to estimate these features.

citation("chronosphere")
To cite chronosphere in publications, use the following citation:

  Kocsis, Á.T., Raja, N.B. 2013 chronosphere: Evolving Earth System
  Variables. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3525481

A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is

  @Misc{,
    title = {chronosphere: Evolving Earth System Variables},
    author = {Ádám T. Kocsis and Nussaibah B. Raja},
    year = {2023},
  }
citation("rgplates")
To cite rgplates in publications, use the following citation:

  Kocsis, Á.T., Raja, N.B., Williams, S., Dowding E.M. (2024).
  rgplates: R interface for the GPlates Web Service and Desktop
  Application. https://zenodo.org/doi/10.5281/zenodo.8093990

  Müller, R. D., Cannon, J., Qin, X., Watson, R. J., Gurnis, M.,
  Williams, S., Pfaffelmoser, T., Seton, M., Russell, S. H. J., &
  Zahirovic, S. (2018). GPlates: Building a Virtual Earth Through Deep
  Time. Geochemistry, Geophysics, Geosystems, 19(7), Article 7.
  https://doi.org/10.1029/2018GC007584

To see these entries in BibTeX format, use 'print(<citation>,
bibtex=TRUE)', 'toBibtex(.)', or set
'options(citation.bibtex.max=999)'.

1. Set up R environment for Paleogeographic reconstructions

# set up list of R packages we need for this
pckgs <- c(
  "rgplates",
  "geojsonsf",
  "chronosphere",
  "via",
  "sf",
  "dplyr",
  "ggplot2",
  "purrr",
  "units",
  "rnaturalearth"
)

# load packages and install them if they are not already there
x <-
  lapply(pckgs, function(pkg) {
    if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE)) {
      install.packages(pkg)
    }
  })
Loading required package: rgplates
Loading required package: sf
Linking to GEOS 3.14.1, GDAL 3.12.1, PROJ 9.7.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE
Loading required package: geojsonsf
Loading required package: chronosphere
Chronosphere - Evolving Earth System Variables
Important: never fetch data as a superuser / with admin. privileges!

Note that the package was split for efficient maintenance and development:
 - Plate tectonic calculations -> package 'rgplates'
 - Arrays of raster and vector spatials -> package 'via'
Loading required package: via

Attaching package: 'via'
The following objects are masked from 'package:sf':

    st_bbox, st_crs, st_transform
Loading required package: dplyr

The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag
The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

Loading required package: units
udunits database from C:/Users/frwo0304/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/cache/v5/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/units/1.0-1/14743a941151a12449e8af67497c7b96/units/share/udunits/udunits2.xml
Loading required package: rnaturalearth
rm(x)

2. Download Paleo-Data

chronosphere is a package that provides access to many version-controlled databases.

Here I use it to retrieve the paleo data. Specifically, I download the paleo coastlines to ensure that we can measure the actual reconstructed landmass areas instead of just the rotated present-day areas. Luckily we can get them from the PALEOMap database.

paleo_coasts <-
  chronosphere::fetch(
    src = "paleomap",
    ser = "paleocoastlines",
    verbose = FALSE
  )

The PaleoMap database is a collection of shapefiles, so we need to select those that we need for our feature estimation. Here is how it looks: We have “margin” and “coast” as columns in the SfcArray. “margin”-files are the continantal plate margins while “coast”-files are the paleo coastlines that take into account sea level changes. We need the “coast” shapefiles. These come in time-steps of 5 Ma (with some exceptions).

paleo_coasts
class         : SfcArray 
Element properties: 
- class       :  sfc_MULTIPOLYGON sfc 
- geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 
Array properties: 
- dimensions  : 81, 2  (nrow, ncol)
- num. layers : 162
- missing     : 0
- proxy:
     margin            coast            
0   "0Ma_CM_v7.shx"   "0Ma_CS_v7.shx"  
5   "5Ma_CM_v7.shx"   "5Ma_CS_v7.shx"  
10  "10Ma_CM_v7.shx"  "10Ma_CS_v7.shx" 
15  "15Ma_CM_v7.shx"  "15Ma_CS_v7.shx"

535 "535Ma_CM_v7.shx" "535Ma_CS_v7.shx"
ages <- c(
  "80",
  "70",
  "65",
  "60",
  "50",
  "45",
  "40",
  "35",
  "30",
  "25",
  "20",
  "15",
  "10",
  "5",
  "0"
)

# initalize empty list to save the selected time slices
paleo_coasts_list <-
  vector("list", length(ages))

for (i in seq_along(ages)) {
  age <-
    ages[i]

  pc <-
    sf::st_sf(geometry = paleo_coasts[age, "coast"])

  plot(pc, main = age)

  pc["time_slice"] <-
    age
  paleo_coasts_list[[i]] <-
    pc
}

3. Identify Madagascar and Africa in Paleo reconstructions

This is a bit tideous because I cannot automatically match Africa and Madgaascar to the shapefiles through time. Instead I’m exploding the multiploygons from the shapefiles into single polygons, assing them individual ids, plot each reconstruction colored by polygon id and extract the polygon_ids for Africa and Madagascar. For Africa I can automatize it [WIP]

# Africa automatization:
world <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(
  scale = "large",
  returnclass = "sf"
)
sf_use_s2(TRUE)
africa_now <- world %>%
  filter(continent == "Africa" & admin != "Madagascar") %>%
  sf::st_make_valid() %>%
  summarise(region = "Africa") %>%
  sf::st_transform(6933) %>%
  sf::st_buffer(100 * 1000) %>%
  sf::st_transform(4326) %>%
  sf::st_make_valid()
afr_list <- vector("list", length(paleo_coasts_list))
sf_use_s2(FALSE)
Spherical geometry (s2) switched off
for (i in seq_along(paleo_coasts_list)) {
  africa <-
    paleo_coasts_list[[i]] %>%
    sf::st_cast("POLYGON") %>%
    sf::st_intersection(africa_now) %>%
    mutate(area = as.numeric(sf::st_area(geometry)) / 1000) %>%
    arrange(desc(area)) %>%
    mutate(poly_id = row_number()) %>%
    slice_max(area) %>%
    summarize(region = "Africa", time_slice = unique(time_slice))

  afr_list[[i]] <-
    africa

  plot(africa)
  title(i)
}
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersection assumes that they
are planar

all geometries

although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are

# Fix the first time slice where Africa is separated into two parts
afr_list[[1]] %>% plot()
africa <-
  paleo_coasts_list[[1]] %>%
  sf::st_intersection(africa_now) %>%
  sf::st_cast("POLYGON") %>%
  mutate(area = as.numeric(sf::st_area(geometry)) / 1000) %>%
  arrange(desc(area)) %>%
  mutate(poly_id = row_number()) %>%
  slice_head(n = 2) %>%
  summarize(region = "Africa", time_slice = unique(time_slice))
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersection assumes that they
are planar
Warning: attribute variables are assumed to be spatially constant throughout
all geometries
Warning in st_cast.MULTIPOLYGON(X[[i]], ...): polygon from first part only
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are
planar
plot(africa)
afr_list[[1]] <-
  africa

Madagascar:

# Empty vector
mda_list <-
  vector("list", length(paleo_coasts_list))
# chose list element
i <- 1

# extract list element
pc <-
  paleo_coasts_list[[i]] %>%
  sf::st_cast("POLYGON") %>%
  mutate(poly_id = row_number())
# plot zoomed map with ids
ggplot(pc) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(poly_id)), color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_sf_text(aes(label = poly_id), size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "poly_id") +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-30, 80),
    ylim = c(-50, 45),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_bw()
Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(sf::st_zm(x)): st_point_on_surface may not
give correct results for longitude/latitude data
# chose id
id <- 30
# check plot
pc %>%
  filter(poly_id == id) %>%
  plot()
# save to list
mda_list[[i]] <-
  pc %>%
  filter(poly_id == id) %>%
  summarize(region = "Madagascar", time_slice = unique(time_slice))
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are
planar
# chose list element
i <- 2

# extract list element
pc <-
  paleo_coasts_list[[i]] %>%
  sf::st_cast("POLYGON") %>%
  mutate(poly_id = row_number())
# plot zoomed map with ids
ggplot(pc) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(poly_id)), color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_sf_text(aes(label = poly_id), size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "poly_id") +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-30, 80),
    ylim = c(-50, 45),

ggplot(pc) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(poly_id)), color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_sf_text(aes(label = poly_id), size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "poly_id") +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-30, 80),
    ylim = c(-50, 45),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_bw()
Warning in st_point_on_surface.sfc(sf::st_zm(x)): st_point_on_surface may not
give correct results for longitude/latitude data
id <- 37

pc %>%
  filter(poly_id == id) %>%
  plot()
mda_list[[i]] <-
  pc %>%
  filter(poly_id == id) %>%
  summarize(region = "Madagascar", time_slice = unique(time_slice))
although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are
planar
i <- 9

pc <- paleo_coasts_list[[i]] %>%
  sf::st_cast("POLYGON") %>%
  mutate(poly_id = row_number())

ggplot(pc) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(poly_id)), color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_sf_text(aes(label = poly_id), size = 3, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(name = "poly_id") +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(-30, 80),
    ylim = c(-50, 45),

planar
# let's save this for later reference
madagascar_extraction_df <-
  data.frame(
    time_slice = seq(1:15),
    id = c(30, 40, 25, 46, 74, 64, 62, 37, 37, 28, 34, 40, 34, 39, 58),
    ages
  )

4. Calculate Area and Distances

To get more or less accurate estimations of area and distance we have to project the data to an equal area projection (spherical)

# our two lists are:
# mda_list
# afr_list

aea <- 6933

sf_use_s2(TRUE) # use spherical geometry package
Spherical geometry (s2) switched on
results <- vector("list", length(ages))

for (i in seq_along(ages)) {
  age <- ages[i]

  message("Processing ", age, " Ma")
  # Area

  ## Africa:

  africa_i_ea <- afr_list[[i]] %>%
    sf::st_transform(aea)

  africa_area_km2 <-
    africa_i_ea %>%
    st_area() %>%
    as.numeric() /
    1e6

  ## Madagascar

  madagascar_i_ea <- mda_list[[i]] %>%
    sf::st_transform(aea)

  madagascar_area_km2 <-
    madagascar_i_ea %>%
    st_area() %>%
    as.numeric() /
    1e6

  # Distance

  ## Distance between nearest points:
  nearest <-
    st_nearest_points(africa_i_ea, madagascar_i_ea)

  ## Plot the distance:
  plot(st_geometry(africa_i_ea), col = "grey")
  plot(st_geometry(madagascar_i_ea), col = "forestgreen", add = TRUE)
  plot(nearest, col = "red", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)

  ## Convert to km
  nearest_km <- as.numeric(st_length(nearest)) / 1000

  # save to results dataframe
  results[[i]] <-
    data.frame(
      age_ma = age,
      africa_area_km2 = africa_area_km2,
      madagascar_area_km2 = madagascar_area_km2,
      nearest_km = nearest_km
    )
}
Processing 80 Ma
Processing 70 Ma
Processing 65 Ma
Processing 60 Ma
Processing 50 Ma
Processing 45 Ma
Processing 40 Ma
Processing 35 Ma
Processing 30 Ma
Processing 25 Ma
Processing 20 Ma
Processing 15 Ma
Processing 10 Ma
Processing 5 Ma
Processing 0 Ma
results_df <- bind_rows(results)
results_df
   age_ma africa_area_km2 madagascar_area_km2 nearest_km
1      80        15244979            467249.1   802.0510
2      70        18550722            485071.8   457.1242
3      65        20411427            425103.4   515.0815
4      60        22322590            492961.8   448.3698
5      50        23066095            502815.0   489.4111

8      35        26792027            566669.7   387.1685

10     25        28338474            552214.2   400.2902
11     20        27919346            512462.6   451.4993
12     15        28825734            566199.2   405.9195

14      5        29212990            565691.1   417.4973
15      0        28812038            509040.8   447.4678

5. Convert to FIG-model format

  1. Age_summary.csv
age_summary <-
  data.frame(
    index = seq(from = 15, to = 1),
    end_age = as.numeric(results_df$age_ma),
    feature_dir = paste0("time", seq(from = 15, to = 1))
  ) %>%
  arrange(index)


age_summary2 <-
  age_summary %>%
  data.frame(
    start_age = age_summary$end_age + 5
  ) %>%
  mutate(mean_age = (age_summary$end_age + start_age) / 2)


age_summary2 <-
  age_summary2 %>%
  select(index, mean_age, start_age, end_age, feature_dir)
write.csv(
  age_summary2,
  here::here("data/output/paleo/age_summary.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
  1. qualitative within-region area size qw_feature1.csv and log-transformed qw_feature2.csv
for (i in seq_along(ages)) {
  dir.create(paste0(here::here("data/output/paleo//"), "time", i))
}

qw = qualitative within-region feature (area) qw1 = untransformed area qw2 = log(area)

results_df2 <-
  results_df %>%
  mutate(index = seq(from = 15, to = 1)) %>%
  arrange(index)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(results_df2))) {
  out1 <- data.frame(
    A = results_df2$africa_area_km2[i],
    M = results_df2$madagascar_area_km2[i]
  )

  write.csv(
    out1,
    file = paste0(
      here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
      "time",
      i,
      "/",
      "qw_feature1.csv"
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )

  out2 <-
    data.frame(
      A = log(results_df2$africa_area_km2[i]),
      M = log(results_df2$madagascar_area_km2[i])
    )

  write.csv(
    out2,
    file = paste0(
      here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
      "time",
      i,
      "/",
      "qw_feature2.csv"
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
}
  1. qualitative between-region distance qb_feature1.csv and log-transformed qb_feature2.csv qb = qualitative between-region feature (distance) qb1 = untransformed distance qb2 = log(distance)
results_df2 <-
  results_df %>%
  mutate(index = seq(from = 15, to = 1)) %>%
  arrange(index)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(results_df2))) {
  out1 <- data.frame(
    A = c(1, results_df2$nearest_km[i]),
    M = c(results_df2$nearest_km[i], 1)
  )
  print(out1)
  write.csv(
    out1,
    file = paste0(
      here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
      "time",
      i,
      "/",
      "qb_feature1.csv"
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )

  out2 <- data.frame(
    A = log(c(1, results_df2$nearest_km[i])),
    M = log(c(results_df2$nearest_km[i], 1))
  )

  write.csv(
    out2,
    file = paste0(
      here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
      "time",
      i,
      "/",
      "qb_feature2.csv"
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
}

Categorical variables 1. cw1 = Age Class (0 = old, 1 = young) 2. cb1 = Dispersal Class (0 = short, 1 = long) 3. cw2 = Growth Class (0 = decay, 1 = growth) 4. cb2 = Relative age class (0 = older, 1 = younger)

results_df2 <-
  results_df %>%
  mutate(index = seq(from = 15, to = 1)) %>%
  arrange(index)

tibble(results_df2)

  1. cw1 should be 1 for all areas and time slices…
out <- data.frame(
  "A" = 1,
  "M" = 1
)

for (i in seq_along(ages)) {
  write.csv(
    out,
    file = paste0(
      here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
      "time",
      i,
      "/",
      "cw_feature1.csv"
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
}
  1. cb1 should be dependent on oceanic currents… to do: read up on currents and timings to make a more informed choice!! (26-06-2026)

…until then let’s go with distance-based. (short if below 401 km - just to see if there is an influence on the outcome)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(results_df2))) {
  time <- results_df2[i, ]

  if (time$nearest_km > 401) {
    out1 <- data.frame(
      A = c(0, 1),
      M = c(1, 0)
    )

    print(out1)

    write.csv(
      out1,
      file = paste0(
        here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
        "time",
        i,
        "/",
        "cb_feature1.csv"
      ),
      row.names = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    out2 <- data.frame(
      A = c(0, 0),
      M = c(0, 0)
    )

    write.csv(
      out2,
      file = paste0(
        here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
        "time",
        i,
        "/",
        "cb_feature1.csv"
      ),
      row.names = FALSE
    )
  }
}

  1. cw2 will depend only on the area size
for (i in seq_len(nrow(results_df2))) {
  time_i <-
    results_df2[i, ]
  time_i_2 <-
    results_df2[i + 1, ]

  Afr <-
    ifelse(time_i$africa_area_km2 - time_i_2$africa_area_km2 < 0, 0, 1)
  Mda <-
    ifelse(time_i$madagascar_area_km2 - time_i_2$madagascar_area_km2 < 0, 0, 1)

  out <-
    data.frame(Afr, Mda)

  if (i == 15) {
    out <- data.frame(Afr = 1, Mda = 1)
  }

  print(out)

  write.csv(
    out,
    file = paste0(
      here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
      "time",
      i,
      "/",
      "cw_feature2.csv"
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
}
  Afr Mda

  1. cb2 = Relative age class (0 = older, 1 = younger) … should both be 0
out <- data.frame(
  "A" = c(1, 1),
  "M" = c(1, 1)
)

for (i in seq_along(ages)) {
  write.csv(
    out,
    file = paste0(
      here::here("data/output/paleo//"),
      "time",
      i,
      "/",
      "cb_feature2.csv"
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
}

Make the feature_summary.csv

feature_df <- expand.grid(
  time_index = 1:15,
  feature_index = 1:2,
  feature_relationship = c("within", "between"),
  feature_type = c("categorical", "quantitative"),
  KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

feature_df$prefix <- with(
  feature_df,
  paste0(
    ifelse(feature_type == "categorical", "c", "q"),
    ifelse(feature_relationship == "within", "w", "b")
  )
)

feature_df$feature_path <- with(
  feature_df,
  paste0(
    "./data/output/paleo/time",
    time_index,
    "/",
    prefix,
    "_feature",
    feature_index,
    ".csv"
  )
)

feature_df$prefix <- NULL

write.csv(
  feature_df,
  file = here::here("data/output/paleo/feature_summary.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE,
  quote = FALSE
)
